answer:
Asana supports three primary authentication methods: Personal Access Tokens (PATs), OAuth 2.0, and Service Accounts [1]. 

PATs are the simplest method, ideal for individual use in scripts, command-line utilities, or prototyping [2][3]. You generate them via the Developer Console and provide them in the Authorization header as a bearer token [4][2].

```shell
curl https://app.asana.com/api/1.0/users/me \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN"
```

OAuth 2.0 is the preferred method for applications that support multiple users or require third-party access without sharing credentials [5][3]. It involves an authorization code grant flow using endpoints at `https://app.asana.com/-/oauth_authorize` and `https://app.asana.com/-/oauth_token` [5]. 

Service Accounts are an Enterprise-tier feature providing org-wide, programmatic access for specific automation needs [1].
